speaker: Andrej Karpathy
title: Software Is Changing Again Software 3.0
why_chosen: Karpathy's June 2025 YC AI Startup School keynote framing Software 1.0/2.0/3.0 paradigm shift. The whole talk IS a paradigm-shift articulation, exactly the input distribution paradigm_shift_finder targets. Recent enough to plausibly contain post-cutoff content.

speaker: Andrej Karpathy
title: Building Software 2.0 Stack
why_chosen: Karpathy 2018 talk on Software 2.0 stack. Predicts compilation pipeline shift from human-written to gradient-descent-written code. Multi-year vision content; many predictions can now be retrospectively tested.

speaker: Richard Sutton
title: Bitter Lesson
why_chosen: Sutton's articulation of his 2019 essay 'The Bitter Lesson' — the canonical first-principle for AI: general methods that scale with compute beat domain-engineered ones. Should produce first_principle atoms.

speaker: Richard Sutton
title: Father of RL thinks LLMs are dead end
why_chosen: Sutton's later argument that LLMs are not a path to AGI; predicts experience-based learning replaces token-prediction. Vision content with explicit predictions and blocker articulation.

speaker: Demis Hassabis
title: Why AGI is bigger than industrial revolution where are bottlenecks
why_chosen: Hassabis on AGI timeline (50% by 2030), bottlenecks, and DeepMind strategy. Bottlenecks framing should yield blocker atoms. Industrial-revolution analogy explicit.

speaker: Yann LeCun
title: Special Lecture AI World Models
why_chosen: LeCun's world-model thesis: LLMs are dead end, JEPA / world models are path forward. Post-cutoff (April 2026) so likely contains content beyond Claude's training. Strong prediction + first_principle content.

speaker: Yann LeCun
title: Self-Supervised Learning JEPA World Models future of AI
why_chosen: LeCun September 2025 lecture on JEPA + world models. Sets up future-of-AI prediction with technical first-principle scaffolding (energy-based models).

speaker: Geoffrey Hinton
title: Will AI outsmart human intelligence
why_chosen: Hinton on superintelligence, digital vs biological intelligence. Strong analogy content (biological computation vs digital) and prediction content (timelines).

speaker: Geoffrey Hinton
title: Martin Lecture Boltzmann Machines
why_chosen: Hinton 2025 Nobel-laureate lecture connecting statistical physics to neural networks. Strong first_principle content (energy-based models, equilibrium principles).

speaker: Stephen Wolfram
title: From Fundamental Physics to AI Computational Universe
why_chosen: Wolfram on computational reducibility and the connection between physics and AI. Heavy first_principle content and unusual cross-domain analogies (physics ↔ ML).

speaker: Stephen Wolfram
title: ChatGPT Natural Language Physics
why_chosen: Wolfram on natural language as computation; predicts shift toward computational language as ChatGPT scaffolding. Strong prediction + first_principle pair.
